text: Amazing what a bit of green can do for sentiment and energy. Yesterday was full of great content and activity across the community and that was just a glimpse of what’s coming once sentiment properly turns. Whether that shift happens later this year or in early 2026, it will happen as I fully expect Bitcoin to trend higher over the long run - this is the anchor to keep coming back to. Even if Smarter Web did nothing from here (which it won’t, but that’s effectively what the current market price suggests), all you’d need is patience. That alone would be enough. It’s easier said than done, but still reassuring. There are short, medium and long-term levers to keep growing Bitcoin per share levers which will be refined as we navigate future cycles. Let’s not forget: we’re only six months into a 10-year mission. Look how much we’ve already learned and accomplished - there’s every reason to stay constructive. Onwards and upwards, together.

text: Thanks for the update, Andrew. I know it’s been a challenging period for everyone across the sector, but your consistent weekly updates and transparency make a real difference. They help reassure all stakeholders that, despite the pressure on the share price and broader market sentiment, the team is working tirelessly to raise awareness of SWC and keep Bitcoin yield moving in the right direction. It’s also clear from the comments that there remains strong and ongoing support for SWC which to me is fantastic to see and extremely motivating - despite this tough time, everyone is showing up for each other. From my perspective, it’s simple- once Bitcoin begins to move higher (and sentiment follows), confidence across the sector will turn. For me, it’s not a question of if, but when.

text: If you borrow from a custodian that’s lending (as an example) and there is no Rehypothecation the collateral never leaves the custodian

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time

text: Lenders have different terms depending whether you allow them to Rehypothecate the Bitcoin (or asset). If you do, the rate tends to be cheaper and some borrowers are happy with this risk. Most instos that borrow don’t want their collateral to be rehyped and state that and the terms of the loan are based on that (amongst other criteria).

text: We may still see some residual weakness in traditional equities next week, given the scale of Friday’s moves and the broader ripple effects. We have to be prepared for that but recognise if it does happen it’s likely to be temporary. I genuinely believe the recent liquidation event, reportedly the largest in history, could mark a bottom for the heavy sentiment that has been weighing on Bitcoin, the broader crypto market, and treasury companies. Others have echoed this view as well. With leverage largely flushed out, the market has an opportunity to reset that will allow Bitcoin to begin to move higher (and $SWC in tandem).

text: Thanks for the update, you and Jesse are doing a fantastic job. I challenge anyone to step into your shoes and match the level of energy, commitment, and resilience you’ve shown. I’m also fully aware there must be a huge amount happening behind the scenes that most of us never see, and I want to acknowledge the time and sacrifice that takes. I truly believe the mission to orange pill the UK is at the heart of what Smarter Web is here to do. It’s not just important, it’s worth fighting for. Once SWC starts making its way into major tracker funds, it has the potential to transform the financial lives of millions. We need that in the UK. I said it earlier this week but it’s unrealistic to expect every CEO moment to be a perfect 10/10 at all times - we have to show empathy. What matters far more is the intention and effort to show up, be visible, and represent the mission across all stakeholders and the community. That’s clearly what you’re doing, and we see it. Yes, the share price is tough, but it’s not unique to us; it’s the entire sector. Could things get worse in the short term? Possibly. But one thing I remain certain of: Bitcoin will rise over the long term and so too will SWC by extension, even if it did nothing else from here. It’s a matter of timing and patience. Sentiment will return. I hope you’re able to get some rest this weekend. Don’t hesitate to reach out to comrades like Saylor who’ve been through similar battles and know that there are thousands of us behind you and Smarter Web, equally committed to its long term success. Onwards and upwards, together.

text: Open letter to @asjwebley Hi Andrew, Just wanted to say congratulations on the recent fundraise. I know there’s been two way discussion around the warrants but I think the structure strikes a smart balance, it’s a win-win and sends a strong positive signal. Raising US capital in this environment is no small feat especially given $SWC is also less than 6 months old. As $SWC continues to grow, you’ll inevitably face differing opinions and criticism (just as Saylor and Gerovich have) but I want you to know you have my full support. I’ve seen similar pushback around the use of ATMs and whether it adds value, but again, I disagree with that view. In my opinion, it’s the right tool for where you are in the growth cycle. Moreover, no CEO is perfect - it’s unrealistic to expect anyone to consistently make the right call at the right time in such a fast-moving industry. Hindsight is a wonderful thing. I think, in general, we need to show empathy toward strong leaders in this space who are doing their best under complex and rapidly evolving conditions. You’re clearly working around the clock, and I can imagine how tough it can be navigating a role like this in a market like today’s. Keep doing what you’re doing, block out the noise, and know that you’ve got me, and plenty of others behind you. Please let @Croesus_BTC know that I think he's also doing a fantastic job and you both have been so visible on various livestreams and engagements which is greatly appreciated. Onwards and upwards together!

text: Bullish note from Tennyson Securities - Maintain BUY The UK’s flagship Bitcoin Treasury company, The Smarter Web Company has raised £12.24m in fresh equity. This included £9.68m institutional placing at 100p per share and £2.56m from its fortnightly “ATM”-like subscription agreement at 102p per share. The institutional placing was dominated by a single U.S.-based investor subscribing for £8.0m and included 1 warrant per share, valid for 36 months and exercisable at 150p (a 50% premium to issue price). Since its IPO in April, SWC has raised over £220m (net), with 94% deployed into Bitcoin purchases. The company now holds 2,525 BTC, valued at £233m, compared to an acquisition cost of £208m, generating a gain of £24.7m as Bitcoin reaches an all-time high above £92,000. Fresh institutional appetite may help revive the flywheel effect, scaling Bitcoin per share more rapidly. The ongoing ATM facility provides a competitive advantage, enabling equity raises at least fortnightly to fund further BTC purchases. With today’s funds, SWC is expected to announce its first BTC purchase of the quarter imminently, strengthening its position in the “BTC yield race.” The stock continues to trade at a healthy mNAV premium and we maintain our BUY recommendation.

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time

text: Big news for $SWC this morning. A major milestone - fantastic to see a large U.S. institutional investor come on board. I believe this will pave the way for others to follow once they see the conviction and traction. At the same time, proceeds from the ATM raise now give us the capacity to acquire 100+ BTC, helping us climb higher on the Bitcoin 100 list and further establish $SWC as the UK’s leading treasury company. Huge congratulations to @asjwebley , @Croesus_BTC , and the entire team. Raising capital in this kind of market is no small feat - it’s a real testament to their belief in the mission and the long-term vision for $SWC . This injection of momentum couldn’t come at a better time, with Bitcoin showing strength and October historically being one of its best-performing months. Onwards and upwards, together. 🚀
